title: The Beacon.
content:
The silent shepherdess,
         She of my vows,
     Here with me exchanging love
         Under dim boughs.

     Shines on our mysteries
         A sudden spark, 
     "Dout the candle, glow-worm,
         Let all be dark.

     "The birds have sung their last notes,
         The Sun's to bed,
     Glow-worm, dout your candle."
         The glow-worm said:

     "I also am a lover;
         The lamp I display
     Is beacon for my true love
         Wandering astray.

     "Through the thick bushes
         And the grass comes she
     With a heartload of longing
         And love for me.

     "Sir, enjoy your fancy,
         But spare me harm,
     A lover is a lover,
         Though but a worm."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert von Ranke Graves, titled "The Beacon.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
